rationale: The citing paper’s focus (scale discriminability effects in conjoint) and the cited paper’s focus (private-label imitation and legal harm) are not directly aligned, which weakens confidence that this is a core substantive citation. But both notes reference hierarchical Bayes and consideration/screening (consider-then-choose) modeling, so the cited article could plausibly be a methodological source. Given the raw evidence is only a DOI match with no in-text citation context, this cannot be safely dropped.
